content: DDI severity classification: Contraindicated (major toxicity expected, avoid combination); Major (potential serious outcome, usually avoid but may use with monitoring/modification); Moderate (effect may be noticeable, consider modification); Minor (minimal clinical significance). Interaction mechanism determination: timing helps distinguish PK (delayed onset as levels change) vs PD (immediate effect). In vitro studies predict interactions using recombinant enzymes, hepatocytes, transporter assays. Clinical DDI studies measure AUC/Cmax changes in presence of perpetrator drug. Regulatory guidance requires DDI evaluation during drug development for new molecular entities. Real-world evidence from pharmacovigilance databases identifies rare interactions not detected in trials.
